candidates.basis: AeroGraphNet surface graph outputs should be invariant/equivariant to row ordering if all graph tensors and surface attributes are permuted consistently.
candidates.decision_reason: Pure representation relabeling is exact if official graph tensors expose row-order semantics.

execution_gate.reason: No raw production outputs or metric ledgers exist yet; Pure representation relabeling is exact if official graph tensors expose row-order semantics.

candidates.metric: relative L2 by surface field channel
candidates.output_mapping: Inverse-permute follow-up surface pressure/wall-shear predictions to source node order.
preconditions: Faces/edges and node features are permuted consistently

candidates.basis: Vehicle geometries and yaw/inlet conventions are often not exactly mirror symmetric; a mirror transform can invalidate geometry or flow orientation.
candidates.decision_reason: Use only as a domain-violation/OOD-stress candidate unless exact geometry and flow symmetry are certified.

execution_gate.reason: No raw production outputs or metric ledgers exist yet; Use only as a domain-violation/OOD-stress candidate unless exact geometry and flow symmetry are certified.

candidates.metric: mapped surface pressure/wall-shear relative L2 plus integrated coefficient drift
candidates.output_mapping: Reflect surface coordinates/normals and map vector wall-shear components if geometry supports it.
preconditions: Flow direction/yaw and boundary metadata are transformed consistently
Surface normals and vector outputs are mapped correctly

candidates.basis: If pressure/wall-shear and surface normals are available, integrated force/drag consistency can check output aggregation but not solver truth.
candidates.decision_reason: Requires official output semantics, mesh areas, and calibrated integration floor before execution.

execution_gate.reason: No raw production outputs or metric ledgers exist yet; Requires official output semantics, mesh areas, and calibrated integration floor before execution.

candidates.metric: absolute/relative drift in drag or force coefficient
candidates.output_mapping: Integrate mapped surface quantities over the same mesh and compare coefficient drift.
preconditions: Pressure or wall-shear output semantics are documented
Drag direction and units are known

candidates.basis: Changing tessellation or geometry without preserving the model's preprocessing contract can create a new design case rather than a metamorphic follow-up.
candidates.decision_reason: Uncontrolled geometry/preprocessing changes are contract violations, not exact MRs.

execution_gate.reason: No raw production outputs or metric ledgers exist yet; Uncontrolled geometry/preprocessing changes are contract violations, not exact MRs.

candidates.output_mapping: Only compare after the official preprocessing map defines correspondence between source and follow-up surfaces.
preconditions: Boundary and feature normalization contracts are unchanged

candidates.basis: Estimate relation-specific floating-point/operator floor from repeated production inference before assigning tolerances.
candidates.decision_reason: Numerical floor cannot be calibrated before official runtime and raw repeated outputs exist.

execution_gate.reason: No raw production outputs or metric ledgers exist yet; Numerical floor cannot be calibrated before official runtime and raw repeated outputs exist.

candidates.metric: repeat-run relative L2 / max absolute difference by output channel
candidates.output_mapping: Compare repeated source-output tensors or surface fields in the model's native output schema.
preconditions: At least two repeated source inferences can be saved as raw outputs

honesty_boundary: This is a candidate ledger only. No production MR has been executed; Task 3--5 workflow results remain blocked until official runtime/checkpoints/data/API plus raw outputs and metric ledgers exist.
